response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack engineer with strong testing practices and some distributed systems exposure, currently at a Staff level at Google, but falls critically short on the role's core requirement: 8 years of C++ experience. The resume shows only a ~6-month stint at National Instruments writing C/C++ components and a bachelor thesis project using C++; C++ does not even appear in the skills section. The role centers on owning a cloud compute platform at the infrastructure layer, while the candidate's background is predominantly web, mobile, and developer tooling. The high-throughput microservices work at Sociomantic is a partial data point in favor, but it does not offset the C++ deficit or the absence of cloud infrastructure architecture and compute-layer leadership.
strengths: Strong testing culture and TDD advocacy demonstrated through open-source libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial) and public workshops
Distributed systems exposure at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async I/O design
Staff-level tenure at Google with contributions to AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling
concerns: Critical C++ gap: role requires 8 years minimum; resume evidences only ~6 months at National Instruments plus a thesis project, with C++ absent from the skills list
No cloud compute platform or cloud infrastructure architecture experience at the depth or scale the role demands
No demonstrated technical leadership over cloud or DevOps engineering teams, a preferred and practically necessary qualification for this role
